metadata.title: A Robust Test for the Stationarity Assumption in Sequential Decision Making
organizations: [1] University of Michigan [2] London School of Economics and Political Science

sections.title: Preliminary
content: Sequential Decision Making
Markov Decision Process (MDP): {pt(St+1, Rt| At, St)}t
Stationarity assumption:H0: {pj(Sj+1, Rj| Sj, Aj)}0≤j≤T-1are homogeneous over time j.
Offline reinforcement learning (RL): static dataset without further interactions.
Additional challenges in modern RL problems:State variable Stcan be high-dimensional (e.g., 7056 for Atari).Dynamics ptcan be highly complex (e.g., autonomous driving).
State variable Stcan be high-dimensional (e.g., 7056 for Atari).
Dynamics ptcan be highly complex (e.g., autonomous driving).
Modern ML methods well-suited for non-linearity and high-dimensionality, however, need careful treatment for proper statistical inference.

figures.description: Markov Decision Process (MDP) interaction diagram showing Agent, Environment, Action, Reward, and State transitions over time t-1, t, and t+1.

sections.title: Proposed Test
content: Graphical illustration
The proposed test is based onsample splitting[1].

figures.description: Graphical illustration of a change point at time t, showing distribution shifts in state transitions and the integral of the difference between expectations.

content: Maximum-type test statistic:
where ψiinvolves aplug-in termand anaugmented termto achieve double robustness.
hbis a test function from a rich function class and B is the number of test functions.
Nuisance functions are estimated using the data from I1. One can useflexible machine/deep learning tools.
Gaussian multiplier bootstrap is used to approximate the limiting distribution and compute the p-value.

sections.title: Theoretical Property
content: Double Robustness:Suppose H0holds. For any h ∈ H and t ∈ (0, T-1), we have E[ψi] = 0. Moreover, the equation is doubly robust, i.e., for any estimated nuisance functions, the equality holds as long as either the transition model or the ratio model is correctly specified.

Size:Suppose B = O((NT)c) for any c > 0 and T = O(T) for any t. Under certain regularity conditions including strict stationarity and geometric ergodicity, the convergence speed of nuisance functions satisfies specific rates, then we have:

figures.description: Simulation results showing Size and Power plots against misspecification levels for different models.
Timeline visualization of check points 1, 2, and 3 in an offline dataset with stationary and slide indicators.
Boxplots comparing 'No change point' and 'Three change points' scenarios across different oracle and detected methods.
Grid of plots showing empirical rejection probability vs kappa for state dimensions 1, 10, 20, and 30.

sections.title: Application to Intern Health Study Data
content: Intern Health Study [2]:Wearable and survey data of 1565 medical interns in the U.S. collected for 26 weeks.
The goal is to improve health-related outcomes via mobile-push notifications.
We consider the next week's daily average step count as the reward Rt.

figures.description: P-value plot for Internal Medicine department across different kappa values.
P-value plot for Family Practice department across different kappa values.

sections.title: References
content: [1] V. Chernozhukov et al., "Double/debiased machine learning for treatment and structural parameters," The Econometrics Journal, 2018.[2] T. NeCamp et al., "Assessing real-time moderation for developing adaptive mobile health interventions for medical interns," Journal of Medical Internet Research, 2020.
